extra.reasoning: The property requires that setting rash=true (all other inputs fixed) never lowers acuity. Examining all 12 rules (r1-r12), none of them reference the rash input in their conditions — rash is declared as an input but is never read by any rule, nor by the default. Therefore the computed acuity is a function purely of the other 12 inputs and is completely independent of rash's value. Flipping rash from false to true (with everything else unchanged) leaves the set of matching rules, the priority/tie-break resolution, and the default-application all identical, so acuity is exactly equal before and after. Equal acuity satisfies "no lower than before." Since this holds for every possible input assignment, the monotonicity property holds with no counterexample.
